Supplement Schedule
Beginning April 12, 2025






























TimeSmoothie PreparationSupplement Taking02:30-03:00Prepare Early Shift smoothie  Use pre-mixed dry ingredientsTake Early Shift stand-alone supplements07:30-08:00Prepare Mid-Shift smoothie  Use pre-mixed dry ingredientsTake Mid-Shift stand-alone supplements  Take Life Extension Super K14:30-15:00Prepare Post-Shift smoothie  Use pre-mixed dry ingredientsTake Post-Shift stand-alone supplements  Take Sleep Formula (moved earlier)17:00-17:30No smoothie at this timeTake Before Sleep supplements
For visualization, see Schedule Gantt Chart.
Back to Top

Fruit Preparation System



































TaskMethodFrequencyPurchaseBuy frozen strawberries, bananas, blueberries, and pitted cherriesEvery 2-3 weeksStrawberry-Banana MixCombine frozen strawberries and bananas in 2:1 ratio  Portion into 7 small containers (½ cup each)  Store in freezerWeeklyBlueberry PortionsPortion frozen blueberries into 7 small containers (½ cup each)  Store in freezerWeeklyCherry PortionsPortion frozen pitted cherries into 7 small containers (½ cup each)  Store in freezerWeeklyThawingMove next day’s portions to refrigerator  Or microwave for 15-20 seconds before useDaily
Back to Top

Weekly Preparation System




















DayTaskTime RequiredSunday• Pre-mix 7 containers of Early Shift dry ingredients  • Pre-mix 7 containers of Mid-Shift dry ingredients  • Pre-mix 7 containers of Post-Shift dry ingredients  • Prepare 21 fruit portions for the week  • Fill 7x4 pill organizer for the week20-25 minutesMonday-Saturday• Blend smoothies at designated times  • Take stand-alone supplements at designated times  • Move next day’s fruit from freezer to refrigerator2-3 minutes per smoothie
Back to Top

Supplement Storage Guidelines








































Supplement TypeStorage LocationNotesDry Ingredients (pre-mixed)Cool, dry place in airtight containersLabel clearly with day and shiftFrozen Fruit PortionsFreezer in individual containersThaw overnight in refrigeratorProbioticsRefrigerator (if indicated on bottle)Check label for specific requirementsFish Oil (Triple Omega)Refrigerator after openingExtends shelf life, prevents rancidityProtein PowderCool, dry place away from moistureKeep scoop dry to prevent clumpingPill OrganizerAccessible location near kitchenAway from direct sunlight or heat

index.title: Supplement Plan
index.content:
How This Started
I’m an over-the-road truck driver. That trade comes with well-known, systemic health problems, and diet is near the top of the list. Good food on the road is borderline unavailable — the old truck-stop diners are mostly gone, and what’s left is fast food chains and gas station hot cases. You eat what’s there, or you don’t eat.
The rest of it compounds. Twelve-hour shifts sitting down. Resistance training is mostly theoretical when your gym is a parking lot and your next shift starts at three in the morning. Work schedule, wake schedule, and sleep all slide around on the load you happen to be running. Muscle doesn’t maintain itself when nothing’s asking it to, and systems that don’t get used get quiet.
So in April 2025 I sat down with an AI chatbot and set up a thought experiment: if whole food were completely off the table — not as a wellness choice but as a practical assumption that matches how most of my working days actually go — what would a nutritionally complete supplement regimen even look like? Built around three smoothies a day. Shoppable from Amazon and Walmart. Prepped on weekends.
The goals were never rearranged after that:

Achieve and maintain healthy weight target for age and height, specifically through metabolic engineering, resulting in fat-targeted weight loss with muscle tissue retention, in the absence of resistance training
Support metabolism against the combined drag of age, sedentariness, and an erratic schedule
Kickstart — or hypercharge where I can — systems at risk of going lethargic: circulation, cognition, digestion, sleep. The ones you only notice after they’ve been sliding for a while

The parameters at the time:

55, male, 6’1”, 220 lbs. Sedentary in the extreme
Zero real food assumed. Only supplements. Milk or water, frozen fruit, and seeds and grains carved out as freely available — those survive a truck cab and a small freezer without becoming a logistics problem
Three milk-based smoothies per day, all dry ingredients pre-combined on Sunday
Pills and tablets ground to powder and folded in; capsules opened and extracted. Combined supplements preferred wherever they exist
Completeness target: 100% of everything — macros, micros, phytonutrients, vitamins, minerals, protein, fiber, and anything else a body needs that I’m not thinking of

What’s on the rest of the site is what that experiment turned into.


A note on what this is — and isn’t
What follows is not the plan in its final form. It isn’t even its current form. It’s a snapshot of a system that was, is, and will continue to be under active refinement — and that, as of this writing, is in the middle of wholesale reconstruction following catastrophic data loss.
This is the complete supplement plan as it currently exists. It is not the complete project. The extensive back-end material — guidelines, sourced studies, methodologies, the reasoning behind specific product choices — lives elsewhere and isn’t included here.
One knock-on effect of the data loss and ongoing reconstruction: a few of the more boutique supplement blends may be identified incorrectly in these documents. If something looks wrong or unfamiliar, it probably is, and it’ll get corrected in the next pass.


Documents

Summary — the short version. Start here.
Comprehensive Plan — full daily regimen, schedule, prep system, product list.
Nutritional Analysis — macro/micronutrient breakdown and RDA comparison.
Visualizations — charts and diagrams supporting the analysis.
User Notes — real-world implementation log, equipment notes, open discussion points.


Reading order
If you have a few minutes: read Summary only.
If you have more time, or you’re a practitioner evaluating the plan:

Summary — orient yourself to the overall shape
Comprehensive Plan — understand the actual protocol
Nutritional Analysis — see the numerical justification
User Notes — see how it’s actually going in practice


Plan version 2.0.4 · last updated April 9, 2025.

Introduction
This document provides a comprehensive nutritional analysis of the Supplement Plan (v2.0), with detailed breakdowns of each component, meal, and the overall daily nutritional profile. All nutritional data has been meticulously researched using actual product information to ensure complete accuracy.
The analysis progresses from specific to general:

Individual ingredients and supplements
Combined smoothie and meal compositions
Complete daily nutritional intake

This document serves as the authoritative reference for all nutritional values in the supplement plan and should be consulted before conducting additional research when nutritional data is needed.

Additional active compounds from stand-alone supplements:

Before Sleep Supplements

Back to Top

Daily Aggregate Analysis
Macronutrient Summary
See Macro Pie Chart and Macro vs RDA Bar Chart.

































































MacronutrientDaily Total% of RDA% of Daily CaloriesProtein213g321%53%Carbohydrates105g75%26%- Dietary Fiber40g133%-- Sugars62.5g-16%Fat54g60%30%- Saturated Fat32g160%18%- Monounsaturated Fat10g-6%- Polyunsaturated Fat12g-7%Total Calories1,75888%100%
Analysis: The macronutrient profile features a very high protein intake (213g daily, approximately 0.97g per pound of body weight), which supports muscle preservation despite lack of resistance training. Carbohydrates are moderate at 75% of RDA, with excellent fiber intake (133% of RDA). Fat intake is balanced at 60% of RDA with a strategic distribution of MCT oil for energy. The total caloric intake of 1,758 calories represents a moderate deficit for a sedentary 55-year-old male weighing 220lbs, which supports the secondary goal of weight reduction through fat loss.
Back to Top
Micronutrient Summary
See RDA Radar Chart and RDA Bar Chart. For key source breakdowns, see Vitamin Sources and Mineral Sources.















































































































































































































































MicronutrientDaily Total% of RDAMain SourcesVitamin A690mcg77%Milk, Green Superfood, fruitsVitamin C82mg91%Green Superfood, fruitsVitamin D10.2mcg51%Milk, multivitaminVitamin E4.5mg30%Fish oil, multivitaminVitamin K (total)3100mcg>100%Super K supplement, Green Superfood- Vitamin K12000mcg1667%Super K supplement, Green Superfood- Vitamin K2 (MK-4)1000mcg-Super K supplement- Vitamin K2 (MK-7)100mcg-Super K supplementThiamin (B1)2.5mg208%Multivitamin, protein powderRiboflavin (B2)3.26mg251%Milk, multivitaminNiacin (B3)22mg138%Multivitamin, protein powderVitamin B63.75mg221%Multivitamin, fruits, protein powderFolate400mcg100%Multivitamin, Green SuperfoodVitamin B127.3mcg304%Milk, multivitaminBiotin30mcg100%MultivitaminPantothenic Acid10mg200%Multivitamin, milkCalcium1445mg111%Milk, protein powder, supplementsIron10mg56%Multivitamin, Green SuperfoodPhosphorus1196mg171%Milk, lecithin, protein powderMagnesium600mg143%Magnesium Glycinate, milk, multivitaminZinc15mg136%Calcium/Mg/Zinc supplement, multivitaminSelenium70mcg127%Multivitamin, protein powderCopper0.9mg100%MultivitaminManganese2.3mg100%Multivitamin, fruitsChromium120mcg343%MultivitaminMolybdenum75mcg167%MultivitaminChloride1mg<1%Multivitamin, milkPotassium1100mg23%Milk, fruits, protein powderSodium652mg28%Protein powder, milk, Green SuperfoodCholine1100mg200%Lecithin, eggs in multivitaminIodine150mcg100%Multivitamin, dairyBoron3mg-MultivitaminOmega-3 Fatty Acids880mg55%Triple Omega supplement- EPA270mg-Triple Omega supplement- DHA330mg-Triple Omega supplementOmega-6 Fatty Acids120mg-Triple Omega supplementOmega-9 Fatty Acids240mg-Triple Omega supplementCoQ10100mg-Qunol Ultra CoQ10
Analysis: The micronutrient profile is comprehensive with most nutrients meeting or exceeding RDA levels. Notable strengths include excellent B vitamin levels, comprehensive vitamin K supplementation, and optimal magnesium intake for muscle function. Areas falling significantly below RDA include potassium (23% of RDA), which is difficult to achieve through supplements alone due to regulatory limitations on potassium supplement dosages. Omega-3 intake is adequate but not optimal at 55% of recommended levels.
Back to Top

summary.title: Supplement Plan Summary
summary.content:
Table of Contents

Core Regimen
Key Nutritional Components
Recent Changes
Product Watchlist
Pending Considerations
30-Day Timeline
Related Documents


Core Regimen

Early Shift (03:00-05:00): Protein smoothie with Green Superfood, Lecithin, MCT oil + 5 stand-alone supplements
Mid-Shift (08:00-10:00): Protein smoothie with Green Superfood, Calcium/Mg/Zinc, MCT oil + Life Extension Super K
Post-Shift (15:00-17:00): Protein smoothie with Green Superfood, Lecithin, MCT oil + Sleep Formula taken earlier
Before Sleep (17:00-18:00): Magnesium Glycinate + Potassium tablets

Back to Top

Key Nutritional Components

Protein: 213g daily (321% RDA)
Carbohydrates: 105g daily (75% RDA)
Fat: 54g daily (60% RDA)
Fiber: 40g+ daily (133% RDA)
Vitamin D: 2000 IU daily (333% RDA)
Magnesium: 600mg daily (143% RDA)
Potassium: 1100mg daily (23% RDA)
Vitamin K: 3100mcg daily (K1, MK-4, MK-7 forms)

Back to Top

Recent Changes

Added MCT Oil Powder to Mid-Shift smoothie for consistent energy (v1.1)
Moved Sleep Formula from bedtime to Post-Shift period for better effectiveness (v1.2)
Moved Calcium/Mg/Zinc from Post-Shift to Mid-Shift for better absorption (v1.3)
Added Vitamin K2 MK-7 supplementation for calcium utilization (v1.4)
Switched from NOW Foods K2 MK-7 to Life Extension Super K for comprehensive vitamin K profile (v1.5)

Back to Top

Product Watchlist

Life Extension Super K Elite (desired traits: comprehensive vitamin K complex with 5 forms including K1, MK-4, MK-6, MK-7, and MK-9)

Back to Top

Pending Considerations

Potassium Redistribution: Analyzing optimal timing and distribution of potassium tablets (See Supplement Plan Topic Parking Lot)

Back to Top

30-Day Timeline

Full plan implementation begins April 12, 2025
Legion Triumph Multivitamin to replace Equate at day 30

Back to Top

Related Documents

Comprehensive Supplement Plan
Supplement Plan Nutritional Analysis
Supplement Plan Visualizations
Supplement Plan Changelog
Supplement Plan Topic Parking Lot

Back to Top

user-notes.title: user-notes
user-notes.content:
User Notes (2025-04-12)
Optimized Supplement Preparation Guide
Implementation of v2.0.4 Comprehensive Supplement Plan with Further Optimizations
Common Dry Ingredients (All Smoothies)

















IngredientAmountNutricost Whey Protein Isolate1 scoop (Updated from 2 scoops)Nutricost MCT Oil Powder1 teaspoon
Morning Smoothie - Additional Dry Ingredients

















IngredientAmountAmazing Grass Green Superfood1 scoopNOW Sunflower Lecithin1 tablespoon
Mid-Shift Smoothie - Additional Dry Ingredients













IngredientAmountNo additional dry scooped ingredients-
Post-Shift Smoothie - Additional Dry Ingredients













IngredientAmountNOW Sunflower Lecithin1 tablespoon

Capsules & Powdered-Form Ingredients (Empty contents into respective smoothie mix)
All Smoothies













IngredientAmountNone common to all smoothies-
Morning Smoothie

























IngredientAmountYerba Prima Prebiotic capsules1 capsule (Updated from 3 capsules)Smarter Turmeric Curcumin1 capsuleClean Nutriceuticals Gluco Defend1 capsuleExtra Strength Brain Supplement Nootropic1 capsule
Mid-Shift Smoothie

















IngredientAmountYerba Prima Prebiotic capsules1 capsule (Updated from 3 capsules)Spring Valley Apple Cider Vinegar1 capsule
Post-Shift Smoothie

























IngredientAmountYerba Prima Prebiotic capsules1 capsule (Updated from 4 capsules)Garden of Life Probiotics Prostate+1 capsuleSpring Valley Apple Cider Vinegar1 capsuleMedChoice 12-in-1 Melatonin Sleep Capsules1 capsule

Pills/Tablets Requiring Grinding
Morning Smoothie

















IngredientAmountEquate Multivitamin1 tabletSpring Valley Potassium2 tablets
Mid-Shift Smoothie

















IngredientAmountSpring Valley Calcium/Mg/Zinc1 tabletSpring Valley Potassium2 tablets
Post-Shift Smoothie













IngredientAmountSpring Valley Potassium2 tablets

Standalone Supplements (Liquid Gelcaps & Non-separable Capsules)
Morning Smoothie

















SupplementAmountNature Made Triple Omega1 capsuleQunol Ultra CoQ101 capsule
Mid-Shift Smoothie

















SupplementAmountNature Made Triple Omega1 capsuleLife Extension Super K1 capsule
Post-Shift Smoothie













SupplementAmountNature Made Triple Omega1 capsule
Before Sleep (No smoothie, separate container)

















SupplementAmountDoctor’s Best Magnesium Glycinate3 capsulesSpring Valley Potassium2 tablets

Products in Stock and Used in This Preparation Plan

MedChoice 12-in-1 Melatonin Sleep Capsules (purple label) - Used as “Nature’s Nutrition Sleep Formula”
MedChoice 12-in-1 Nootropic Brain Supplement (blue label) - Not currently used in this preparation plan
MedChoice 4-in-1 Turmeric and Garlic Supplements (copper/golden label) - Not currently used in this preparation plan

Additional Items in Stock but Not Used in This Preparation Plan

Spring Valley Brand:

Glucosamine Sulfate Potassium Chloride 1000mg
Milk Thistle 175mg
Super Vitamin B-Complex
Extra Strength Turmeric Curcumin Compound 1000mg
B-12 1000mcg
D3 25mcg 1000 IU
Turmeric Curcumin 500mg


CVS Health Brand:

Vitamin C 1000mg


Ultra Herbs Brand:

9-in-1 Biotin Niacin Collagen Keratin 14519mg




Preparation Tips

Prepare each type of smoothie as a batch (7 containers of morning, 7 of mid-shift, 7 of post-shift)
Grind tablets in batches by smoothie type to save time
Empty all capsule contents into the appropriate dry mix (except standalone supplements)
Store prepared dry mixes in airtight containers
Place standalone supplements in separate labeled containers
Label all containers clearly with day and time of consumption
Consider implementing color coding system: green-white-red for early-mid-late smoothies


Discussion Topics for Comprehensive Supplement Plan Creator


Brand Identification Updates:

“Nature’s Nutrition Sleep Formula” identified as MedChoice 12-in-1 Melatonin Sleep Capsules
“Cinnamon Turmeric ACV Berberine” identified as Clean Nutriceuticals Gluco Defend



Container Size Issues:

Current containers may be too small for the preparation ingredients
Temperature adjustment is too heavily frontloaded



Lecithin Distribution:

Original plan included lecithin in all 3 smoothies
Current implementation includes it only in morning and evening smoothies



Prebiotic/Probiotic Combination:

Is combining prebiotics and probiotics in the late smoothie optimal?
Would separation provide better benefits?



Grinding Challenges:

Multivitamin is particularly difficult to grind
Need alternative approach or equipment



Amazing Grass Green Superfood Optimization:

Updated: Now only in morning smoothie instead of all three smoothies
Reduces cost while maintaining most benefits



Magnesium Glycinate Availability:

Doctor’s Best Magnesium Glycinate currently not in stock
Need guidance on suitable alternative or waiting for restocking



MedChoice Products Potential:

Have additional MedChoice products that aren’t in the current preparation plan
Could potentially replace some other supplements



Additional Available Supplements:

Several supplements in stock but not in current preparation plan
Opportunity to adjust plan based on actual inventory



Color Coding Implementation:

Proposed green-white-red system for visual identification
Would improve workflow and reduce errors



Protein Amount Reduction: (NEW)

Updated: Reduced to 1 scoop per smoothie instead of 2 scoops
More economical while still providing adequate protein



Yerba Prima Prebiotic Reduction: (NEW)

Updated: Reduced to 1 capsule per smoothie instead of 3-4 capsules
More economical while still providing prebiotic benefits
from the user:
Yeaah, early smoothie tastes like ASS!Added three pumps of vanilla coffee syrup (not the healthiest choice, but it is what it is). Not a remarkably big increase in enjoyment, but it helps aa little. A different flavor may help, as well, though i find vanilla tends to help ‘soften’ overly distinctive flavors



Purchased a different blender. I love the ninja blast max; it’s perfect in every way, but it’s vessel size is simply too small. 18oz max load and 22oz max with headroom, it just explodes to much after aerating the smoothie so well. No other blender I can find is as perfect in the other ways (blades incorporated into base of vessel, making for upright blending instead of inverted, and no need to replace blade attachment with drinking lid attachment reducing mess, complexity, and cleanup), portable/rechargeable and vessel separates from motor/battery base. Sooo perfect! but…vessel size is nonnegotiable. So, purchased a nutribullet pro. 900 watt, requires ac power, unfortunately. 34oz vessel,  though, so it satisfies the vessel size requirement. Reviews on amazon are absolute garbage, though, and nutribullet might be dropping the product based on that (completely guessing, buuuuuut walmart had them on clearance for 25. 25! I, uh…bought two of them)

Related Documents
Change Log


Introduction
This document contains textual descriptions and implementation code for visual representations of the Supplement Plan’s nutritional data, timing, and relationships. These visualizations are designed to provide clear insights into the plan’s composition, nutritional impact, and implementation strategy. All visualizations described or implemented are based on data from the Supplement Plan Nutritional Analysis document.
(Note: Implemented visualizations require specific Obsidian plugins. See Required Plugins List and VIP Reference for details).
The visualizations are organized into categories:

Macronutrient balance and distribution
Micronutrient levels relative to RDAs
Supplement timing and absorption strategies
Component contributions to overall nutrition
Phytonutrient diversity and sources
Cost-benefit analyses for various supplements

Back to Top

Macronutrient Visualizations
Daily Macronutrient Balance
Daily Macronutrient Ratio (% Calories) (Pie Chart)

Macronutrients vs. RDA (%) (Bar Chart)

Related data: Macronutrient Data

Supplement Timing Visualizations
Daily Supplement Schedule
Daily Supplement Schedule (Gantt Chart)
 VIS-TIME-GRAPH-ABSORPTION - Based on Supplement Plan Nutritional Analysis v2.0.3 Absorption Considerations

graph TD
    subgraph Early_Shift [Early Shift]
        ES_Fat[Fat Sources (MCT, Lecithin, Milk)] --> ES_VitA(Vit A)
        ES_Fat --> ES_VitD(Vit D)
        ES_Fat --> ES_VitE(Vit E)
        ES_Ca[Ca (Milk, Protein)]
    end

    subgraph Mid_Shift [Mid-Shift]
        MS_Ca[Ca/Mg/Zn Supp]
        MS_K[Super K (Vit K)]
        MS_Ca -- Synergistic --> MS_K
        MS_Fat[Fat Sources (MCT, Milk)] --> MS_K
        MS_Zn[Zn from Supp]
    end

    subgraph Post_Shift [Post-Shift]
       PS_Fat[Fat Sources (MCT, Lecithin, Milk)]
       PS_Probio[Probiotics]
    end

    subgraph Before_Sleep [Before Sleep]
        BS_Mg[Mg Glycinate]
    end

    %% Interactions / Separations
    MS_Ca -. Antagonistic .-> MS_Zn
    MS_Ca -. Separated From .-> BS_Mg
    MS_Zn -. Separated From .-> BS_Mg

    style Early_Shift fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
    style Mid_Shift fill:#ccf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
    style Post_Shift fill:#ff9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
    style Before_Sleep fill:#cfc,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px

    linkStyle 1 stroke:green,stroke-width:2px;
    linkStyle 3 stroke:green,stroke-width:2px;
    linkStyle 5 stroke:red,stroke-dasharray: 5 5;
    linkStyle 6 stroke:orange,stroke-dasharray: 5 5;
    linkStyle 7 stroke:orange,stroke-dasharray: 5 5;

Change Log

v2.0.4 (April 9, 2025): Replaced descriptive text with implementation code blocks (Mermaid/Chart) for kept visualizations. Marked skipped/pending visualizations. Updated internal links and Related Documents to use canonical filenames. Updated YAML version.
v2.0.1 (April 8, 2025): Fixed Table of Contents formatting and added “Back to Top” navigation links.
v2.0 (April 8, 2025): Initial release of comprehensive visualization document. Created visual representations of all key nutritional aspects of the supplement plan including macronutrient balance, micronutrient achievement, timing strategies, component contributions, phytonutrient diversity, and cost-benefit analyses.

Back to Top
